title: fix `podman -v` regression
body:
Re-add the shortflag for --version and add e2e tests to avoid regressing
in the future.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I think that we had to remove this because Cobra really, strongly disliked having `-podman run -v src:dst:options` and `podman -v` both be possible... Might be misremembering, though

```[+2466s] panic: unable to redefine 'v' shorthand in "rm" flagset: it's already used for "volumes" flag```

Yep, I think that's our culprit here.
Seems like a bug in cobra, but not something we can easily fix.

I read a bit through the docs and found a solution. The issue was that `--version` was marked as a persistent flag, so it was propagated to all subcommands. Making it a local flag solved the issue.

This comes at the cost of `./bin/podman --version run alpine ls` really executing a container and the `--version` being ignored. Personally I prefer the new behaviour.

> All kinds of test unhappiness @vrothberg

That was a brain fart in the tests I added. `s/BeFalse/BeTrue` will fix it :)

Huh. Nice to know we can work around issues like this.

Code LGTM
LGTM but would like a head nod from @baude

This makes sense to me.
Since podman run -v ... continues to work.
/lgtm
